sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check what changed after RIGHT
before = last_transition.before_frame
after = last_transition.after_frame

before_lines = before.ascii.split('\n')
after_lines = after.ascii.split('\n')
changed = []
for i, (bl, al) in enumerate(zip(before_lines, after_lines)):
    for j, (bc, ac) in enumerate(zip(bl, al)):
        if bc != ac:
            changed.append((i, j, bc, ac))
print(f"Changed cells ({len(changed)}):")
for r, c, bc, ac in changed:
    print(f"  [{r},{c}]: '{bc}' -> '{ac}'")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
The left green token (with white center W) moved right by 3 cells. It was at cols 18-20, now at cols 21-23. The white center moved from [31,19] to [31,22]. So RIGHT moves the left green token rightward.
Now let me understand the game:
- There are two green tokens (3x3): one with white center (W), one with black center (B)
- There are two dark gray containers (5x5 with 3x3 white interior)
- The goal is likely to place each token into a container
- Arrow keys move the tokens
Let me figure out which token is "selected" and how to switch between them. Currently the left token moved. Let me try pressing RIGHT again to see if it continues moving, or try other directions.
But first, let me check the current state of the board to see where things are now.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Current state - check token positions
seg = current_frame.segmentation
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] == 'N':
        b = n['boundary']
        minr = min(p[0] for p in b)
        minc = min(p[1] for p in b)
        print(f"Green token id={n['id']} at [{minr},{minc}], children={n['children']}, hash={n['hash'][:8]}")
    if n['color'] == 'c' and n['pixels'] == 16:
        b = n['boundary']
        minr = min(p[0] for p in b)
        minc = min(p[1] for p in b)
        print(f"Container id={n['id']} at [{minr},{minc}], children={n['children']}")

# Also check the white square
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] == 'w' and n['pixels'] == 9:
        b = n['boundary']
        minr = min(p[0] for p in b)
        minc = min(p[1] for p in b)
        print(f"White square id={n['id']} at [{minr},{minc}]")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

Now I can see:
- Green token id=6 (white center) moved from [30,18] to [30,21] (moved right 3 cells)
- Green token id=8 (black center) is at [30,27]
- White square id=7 at [30,24] (this was the old position area, now white)
- White square id=9 at [30,30] (the original white square between tokens)
- Container id=4 at [26,44] (right platform)
- Container id=12 at [32,11] (left platform)
So the left green token moved right by 3 cells. Now it's at [30,21]. The white square that was at [30,30] is still there. There's now a white square at [30,24] which was the old position of the token.
The layout is:
```
Container(32,11) ... Token(30,21) ... White(30,24) ... Token(30,27) ... White(30,30) ... PurpleWall ... Container(26,44)
```
So the tokens are on the same horizontal row. The left container is at [32,11] and the right container is at [26,44].
I need to figure out:
1. How to move tokens into containers
2. Which token goes to which container
3. Whether I can switch between tokens
